title: oenothera glauca f. fraseri
intro: Oenothera glauca f. fraseri, also known as the Fraser's evening primrose, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Onagraceae family. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The plant's extracts and oils have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, offering benefits such as promoting wound healing, reducing inflammation, and fighting off infections. Herbal preparations of Oenothera glauca f. fraseri include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
